acceptance: A stable external file is read through one bounded buffer and returns its verified identity.
An oversized external file is rejected before parser execution.
A same-size file replacement during read is detected through file identity, not size alone.
A source symlink and any source located logically or physically inside the project are rejected.
Project JSON cannot be imported through the Review Packet external-file route.
Save As targets inside the project, matching protected sources, or using a symlink leaf are rejected.
DOCX, Markdown, TXT, and Review Packet exports revalidate their target inside the disk queue before atomic write.
Queue-time replacement of an approved target by a symlink produces zero writes and leaves canonical project bytes unchanged.
Markdown local-file commands reject renderer paths, source text, scene snapshots, apply operations, and recovery references.
Markdown preview and safe-create receipts exposed through the new authority commands contain no filesystem path authority.

machineChecks.command: node --test Phase 03 focused file-authority and adjacent format set

expectedFailures: sector-m diff does not leak outside cumulative phase allowlist
sector-m runner writes fast artifact schema

nonClaims: The Markdown local-file authority commands are not yet bound to the visible renderer product flow.
Markdown export still requires Phase 04 canonical saved-scene or document-IR serialization and semantic golden acceptance.
No Markdown product-flow completion or release gate is claimed by this status.
No DOCX fidelity, tracked-change apply, structural apply, or comment auto-apply is introduced.
No cross-scene or project-wide transaction is introduced.
No cloud, account, authorization, synchronization, or network integration is introduced.
No dependency, UI framework, renderer redesign, CSS change, or HTML structure change is introduced.
Release readiness is not claimed; Markdown completion, discoverability, and capability evidence remain separate phases.
